key_insight: CSP-MACE-Å is a machine-learned interatomic potential that can approach DFT-level accuracy for crystal structure prediction while running orders of magnitude faster.
engineering_relevance: It enables much broader screening of candidate solid forms by making both energy and harmonic free-energy ranking feasible at near-DFT quality.
method: Split total energy into intramolecular and intermolecular parts.
Train the intramolecular model with MACE-POLAR on OMol25.
Add intermolecular dispersion plus a learned delta model fit to B86bPBE-XDM residuals from 50,000 crystal calculations.
evidence_signal: Across 19 AstraZeneca CSP compounds and 28 blind-test compounds, the model matches or nearly matches PBE+Neumann and B86bPBE-XDM performance, with harmonic free-energy reranking improving results.
watch_item: Performance is validated on curated benchmark sets, so robustness on chemically broader or highly unusual crystal chemistries still needs caution.
paper_title: DFT Accuracy on Crystal Structure Prediction with Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials
